title: Ful nabed
subtitle: Egyptian white fava bean salad
excerpt: Dried white split fava beans cooked until just tender, then dressed while warm with spring onion, tomato, lemon, cumin, and olive oil. Served at room temperature as a salad or light breakfast dish. Lighter and fresher than the slow-cooked whole-bean ful medames, a different preparation entirely.
sourceNotes: Ful nabed (broad beans, peeled) uses dried split white fava beans (peeled and halved), which cook faster than whole favas and produce a lighter, less starchy result than ful medames. The preparation is closer to a warm salad than to the thick braised stew of ful medames. It is a distinct dish made from a different product.

content.text: Ful nabed is a warm bean salad made from dried split white fava beans: lighter and faster than ful medames, dressed with lemon, cumin, and olive oil. No soaking needed. The beans go straight into the pot and cook in 25 to 30 minutes. Serve at room temperature as a light breakfast or side dish with bread.
Dress the beans while still warm so they absorb the lemon and oil. Add the spring onion and tomato just before serving: the heat from the beans will wilt them if they go in too early.

Simmer the {{dried-split-fava-beans}} in plenty of salted water for 25 to 30 minutes until just tender but still holding their shape. Check from 20 minutes: they should have a slight bite, not be soft all the way through.
Drain well. While still warm, toss with the {{garlic}}, {{cumin-ground}}, {{lemon-juice}}, {{olive-oil}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}}.
Leave to cool to room temperature for 20 minutes. Stir in the {{spring-onion}}, {{tomato}}, and {{parsley-flat}}.
Taste and adjust salt and lemon. Cool salads need more acid and salt than when warm. Serve with bread.

items.fix: Check from 20 minutes and pull off the heat as soon as each bean is tender throughout. Drain immediately into a sieve and do not leave sitting in the cooking water
Taste again at room temperature. Cool salads need more acid and salt than when warm. Add another squeeze of lemon and a pinch of salt
items.cause: Dressing applied too sparingly or not adjusted after cooling

items.fix: Ful nabed is a textured salad; bessara is a smooth dip. The beans must keep their shape. Stop cooking while there is still a slight bite
items.cause: Beans overcooked until soft rather than salad-tender

content.text: Ful in any form is the Egyptian breakfast. While ful medames uses whole dried brown favas cooked for hours into a thick stew, ful nabed uses white split favas in a lighter, faster preparation closer to a salad. Both are eaten with bread, spring onion, tomato, and a drizzle of olive oil: same accompaniments, different textures and intensities. This is a weekday breakfast dish: quick to cook, filling, and complete.
